doc_title: NUREG-2173, Rev. 1 - Tribal Protocol Manual - July 2018.

steps.text: Familiarize yourself with the treaty history.
Initiate dialogue through written correspondence between the leader of the Tribal government and an appropriate representative of NRC management.
Identify the Tribal contacts, their roles, and positions within the Tribal communities.
Discuss with the Tribal liaison whether the Tribal government considers the meeting privileged or confidential.
Identify the issues, topics, and desired outcomes for the meeting.
Clarify with the Tribal government whether the planned meeting is public or nonpublic.
Identify meeting participants and the appropriate rank of the NRC representative.
Provide an opportunity for the Tribe to present a proposed meeting agenda in advance.
Share the NRC's proposed agenda with the Tribe and include suggestions from both in the final agenda.
Seek guidance from the Office of the General Counsel regarding food/refreshment purchases.
Inform Tribal government representatives of ethical restrictions regarding food and refreshments.
Conduct the meeting, exchanging information and allowing sufficient time for Tribal representatives to caucus.
Allow Tribal meeting participants the opportunity to review the NRC's draft summary.
Follow up with a telephone call to the Tribal contact if a reply to written correspondence is not received within the expected time period.

preconditions.text: When beginning any outreach with Tribes
Before offering food or refreshment at an NRC hosted meeting

constraints.text: Under Federal law, food and refreshments are generally considered to be personal expenses that cannot be purchased using Federal funds.

Standards of Ethical Conduct allow NRC employees to accept modest items of food and refreshments that are not part of a meal, or meals not exceeding $20.00 in value per occasion and $50.00 per year from a single party.
A formal government to government meeting between the NRC and a Tribal government is not a public meeting and should not be made public by the NRC staff.
